title: Qilin and Warlock Ransomware Use Vulnerable Drivers to Disable 300+ EDR Tools
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-07, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FglgW44/1/0100019d680e3e5e-48e9781c-6ed8-4009-baf6-9a49b137a079-000000/eVUzA6Kxkif4aXJomBP-rTtrKhoBEzqyctGyV1geo4s=451
summary: Qilin and Warlock Ransomware Use Vulnerable Drivers to Disable 300+ EDR Tools (3 minute read) Qilin and Warlock ransomware are using Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) tactics to disable over 300 EDR tools, with Qilin specifically dropping a malicious DLL ("msimg32.xml") that loads two signed-but-vulnerable drivers to access physical memory and terminate EDR drivers before deploying their payloads.

title: Google tests Jules V2 agent capable of taking bigger tasks

summary: Google tests Jules V2 agent capable of taking bigger tasks (3 minute read) Google is developing the Jules V2 ("Jitro") coding agent, designed to autonomously manage high-level development goals rather than specific tasks. Launching via waitlist, it aims to redefine AI software development by shifting focus from task-based commands to KPI-driven outcomes. The approach might benefit teams handling large codebases, but it faces challenges of unpredictable changes and trust issues.

summary: AIs can now often do massive easy-to-verify SWE tasks and I've updated towards shorter timelines (30 minute read) There is now an almost 2x higher probability of full AI R&D automation by the end of 2028. Strong performance in easy-and-cheap-to-verify SWE tasks that don't require new ideas will allow AI to substantially speed up AI R&D. A lot of the work in AI R&D involves these types of tasks, and AI can complete them significantly cheaper and faster than humans.

summary: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline (2 minute read) Google AI Edge Eloquent is a free iOS dictation app that turns speech into polished text using on-device AI. title: S3 Files and the changing face of S3
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-08, Reading time: 20 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.allthingsdistributed.com%2F2026%2F04%2Fs3-files-and-the-changing-face-of-s3.html%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019d6cc53cdf-d8ab3adb-84ce-4986-b6e2-202c25d79f97-000000/ACH7WhGKD9eNdnl-HmhL9oh9sgyoDoTR-uy83VLQzjg=452
summary: S3 Files and the changing face of S3 (20 minute read) S3 Files allows S3 data to be accessed as a filesystem by layering a file interface (via EFS) on top of object storage, using a “stage and commit” model that preserves the fundamental differences between file and object semantics instead of forcing them into a single system. This approach reduces data friction by letting data stay in one place while supporting both file-based and object-based access patterns without compromise.

title: S3 Files and the changing face of S3
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-08, Reading time: 31 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.allthingsdistributed.com%2F2026%2F04%2Fs3-files-and-the-changing-face-of-s3.html%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d6cc86177-299f25d8-23df-4e33-803a-47deb1aec1b3-000000/pm3nEdXpDcKrhib1LV9k8UlaIq7aCmAXLqOYNhMj64s=452
summary: S3 Files and the changing face of S3 (31 minute read) AWS S3 Files is a new feature that integrates Amazon EFS with S3 to resolve "data friction" when moving large datasets. It enables S3 buckets to be mounted as network-attached file systems on EC2, containers, or Lambda. Instead of merging file and object semantics, the design uses an explicit "stage and commit" boundary to optimize for distinct access patterns, broadening S3's capabilities beyond simple object storage.

title: Parse, Don't Validate — In a Language That Doesn't Want You To
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-08, Reading time: 12 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcekrem.github.io%2Fposts%2Fparse-dont-validate-typescript%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d6cc86177-299f25d8-23df-4e33-803a-47deb1aec1b3-000000/YzvgUMbG-ytP_x1dSesVRAGw9tI8fLhypmhRNWplaec=452
summary: Parse, Don't Validate — In a Language That Doesn't Want You To (12 minute read) The "Parse, don't validate" principle makes sure of type safety by transforming raw input into precisely typed forms that encode validity directly into the system. This approach establishes a clear boundary between untrusted data and trusted domain types, reducing errors and the need for defensive programming.

summary: Running out of Disk Space in Production (8 minute read) A production server quickly ran out of disk space due to Nginx buffering large files, causing significant system errors and user complaints. summary: Claude Is Not Your Architect. Stop Letting It Pretend (7 minute read) Don't let AI agents make architectural decisions. title: How I Run a Software Engineering Standup

title: Vibe → Environment → Culture: Leadership Gets It Backwards
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-08, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FsVw2Ms/1/0100019d6d1b660e-b97ca0e7-4685-4bb6-9935-649f9c2c2e9e-000000/7ux4Tl8mSiJc5BroOZ0YOLiOauu33nlQ49fGgRUlDw4=452
summary: Vibe → Environment → Culture: Leadership Gets It Backwards (3 minute read) Leaders obsess over culture when they should start with their vibe. summary: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security (4 minute read) OpenClaw recently patched CVE-2026-33579 (CVSS 8.1–9.8), a flaw that allowed anyone with the lowest-level pairing permission to silently escalate to full admin access without user interaction. title: Understanding Traceroute
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-10, Reading time: 16 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftech.stonecharioteer.com%2Fposts%2F2026%2Ftraceroute%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019d7727cfb7-98c15ebb-5c93-4217-b8cf-2b48fe4e8b5f-000000/9aX_6cuZFH0JffwltVDizsFuSVRdpCFI6zt24fBOfLQ=452
summary: Understanding Traceroute (16 minute read) Traceroute works by sending packets with increasing TTL values so each router drops them in sequence and returns an ICMP “Time Exceeded” message, revealing each hop's IP until the destination responds with a different ICMP message. By repeating this process with multiple probes and measuring response times, it maps the path and latency of network traffic, though gaps occur when routers don't send replies.

title: Archon
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-10, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fcoleam00%2FArchon%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019d7727cfb7-98c15ebb-5c93-4217-b8cf-2b48fe4e8b5f-000000/cy5uIG4caAezCRqOYvvBBYKgH-oa_FTMA6046uZY83w=452
summary: Archon (GitHub Repo) Archon is the first open-source workflow engine for AI coding agents. It allows developers to define development processes like planning, testing, and PR creation as deterministic YAML workflows rather than relying on unpredictable AI behavior. The tool, which works with platforms like Claude Code, includes 17 default workflows, a web dashboard for monitoring, and aims to bring the same standardization to AI coding that Dockerfiles brought to infrastructure.
